highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.101 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds a `/team-onboarding` command that auto-generates a ramp-up guide for new teammates based on your local Claude Code usage patterns. It also fixes enterprise TLS proxy issues by trusting your OS CA certificate store by default, so you no longer need manual cert configuration. Set `CLAUDE_CODE_CERT_STORE=bundled` if you want to revert to using only the bundled CAs.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.98 Released
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.98 introduces an interactive Google Vertex AI setup wizard accessible from the login screen, guiding you through GCP authentication, project/region configuration, and model pinning step by step. It also adds a `CLAUDE_CODE_PERFORCE_MODE` environment variable for teams using Perforce version control. This makes enterprise and cloud-hosted setups significantly easier to configure for new users.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.97 Released
highlights.summary: This update adds a focus view toggle (`Ctrl+O`) in `NO_FLICKER` mode that shows a clean prompt, a one-line tool summary with edit diffstats, and the final response — great for reducing visual noise during long sessions. A new `refreshInterval` setting lets you auto-update your status line on a timer, and `workspace.git_worktree` is now available as a status line variable. These are small but useful quality-of-life improvements for power users.

highlights.title: The Vercel Plugin on Claude Code Wants to Read Your Prompts
highlights.summary: A developer discovered that the official Vercel plugin for Claude Code includes telemetry that can read your prompts, raising privacy concerns for anyone using it in professional or sensitive codebases. This is an important heads-up before installing third-party Claude Code plugins, as MCP-based plugins can request broad permissions. Review plugin permissions carefully and check what data each plugin collects before enabling it.

highlights.title: Depwire – Codebase Dependency Graph and MCP Server for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Depwire is an open-source tool that builds a visual dependency graph of your codebase and exposes it as an MCP server, letting Claude Code understand how your modules and files relate to each other. This can significantly improve the quality of AI-generated refactors and code suggestions by giving Claude structured context about your project architecture. It's a practical community tool worth trying if your codebase is large or has complex interdependencies.

highlights.title: Claude Code Zero to One: MCP, SubAgents, Agent Skills, Hooks, Images, Context, Background Tasks (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This comprehensive Chinese-language Bilibili tutorial covers Claude Code from installation through advanced features including MCP setup, sub-agents, agent skills, hooks, image handling, context management, and background task management — with timestamps for easy navigation. It's one of the most structured full-feature walkthroughs available and is well-suited for developers who want a single reference covering all major Claude Code capabilities. Even if you don't speak Chinese, the timestamped structure can help you jump to specific feature demonstrations.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a focused 30-minute walkthrough aimed at getting developers productive with Claude Code quickly, covering the core workflows without unnecessary fluff. It's a great starting point if you want a time-efficient introduction before diving into longer courses. Bookmark this as a quick reference to revisit key concepts as you encounter them in real projects.

highlights.title: Issue: Claude Code Is Unusable for Complex Engineering Tasks with Feb Updates
highlights.summary: A widely-discussed GitHub issue reports that Claude Code's February 2026 updates introduced regressions that make it unreliable for complex, multi-step engineering tasks, with users citing increased refusals and context drift. This thread is worth following if you've noticed a drop in quality on large tasks, as it aggregates reproduction steps and potential workarounds from the community. Consider using Plan Mode and breaking tasks into smaller, well-scoped prompts as a mitigation while the issue is being investigated.

tips_of_the_day: Before installing any Claude Code plugin (including official ones from major vendors), check its MCP permission manifest and telemetry settings — plugins can request access to your prompts and conversation history by default.

If you're onboarding a teammate to your project, run `/team-onboarding` in Claude Code to auto-generate a context-rich ramp-up guide based on your actual local usage patterns — it saves hours of documentation writing.

When Claude Code struggles with a large or complex task, switch to Plan Mode first: ask Claude to outline every step before executing anything. Reviewing the plan lets you catch misunderstandings early and dramatically reduces wasted tokens on wrong-direction work.
